decisions.summary: Scope = Ask #4 (surface tracker-quota exhaustion as a health signal) + regression hardening. Asks #1/#5 already done; #2 split to PAN-1821/PAN-1823; #3 already satisfied (poll is batched paginated GraphQL, no per-issue fan-out).

Persist a per-tracker poll-health signal in a NEW SQLite table `tracker_poll_health` in CacheService. This is the single source of truth read by BOTH the in-process dashboard health service AND the out-of-process `pan doctor` CLI. Required because `pan doctor` is a separate process and cannot see the dashboard's in-memory `state.lastError`.

Detect quota exhaustion REACTIVELY from poll error messages (regex /rate.?limit/i) -> status `quota_exhausted`. This is independent of PAN-1821 (which adds PROACTIVE Linear rate-limit-header parsing). The two compose without conflict.

GitHub uses its already-persisted `rate_limits.remaining` for an early low-quota warning; Linear/Rally use the new reactive poll-health error signal. Do NOT write to `rate_limits` for Linear (PAN-1821 owns that).

Surface the signal in two existing operator-facing places: (a) new `checkTrackerQuota()` row in `pan doctor`; (b) a `warning` severity + `reasons[]` entry in `/api/system/health`, reusing the existing SystemHealthPill/HealthDashboard rendering. No new React component.

Regression hardening = write the MISSING idle-stack-reaper unit test. The poller-gate is already locked by the existing `skipPolling` suite (both branches), so no new poller test is added. `idle-stack-reaper.ts` ships a `__resetIdleStackReaperState()` test hook but has ZERO test files.

hazards.summary: Recording poll health could fire on peer dashboards and create spurious signals.
hazards.mitigation: Recording lives INSIDE pollLinear/pollGitHub/pollRally, which only execute on the host poller. Peers run with skipPolling and never call them. Add an NFR-2 test asserting skipPolling start records nothing.

hazards.summary: A `quota_exhausted` record could become sticky and warn forever after the quota recovers.
hazards.mitigation: Record `ok` on every successful poll (clears the prior error). Store `observed_at`; doctor/health reads treat records older than a staleness window as no-signal. Covered by FR-4 test.

hazards.summary: Overlapping PAN-1821 by writing Linear data into the `rate_limits` table.
hazards.mitigation: Use the dedicated `tracker_poll_health` table only. Never call updateRateLimit('linear', ...) in this issue. NFR-1 test asserts no `rate_limits` write for Linear.

hazards.summary: Cross-process SQLite access: dashboard writes, doctor CLI reads the same cache DB.
hazards.mitigation: CacheService already opens WAL mode. Only the dashboard writes poll health; the doctor CLI is read-only. No new locking concern.

hazards.summary: Linear rate-limit error string format may vary; a brittle exact-match would miss it.
hazards.mitigation: Classify with a case-insensitive /rate.?limit/i regex on err.message, not an exact string. Unit-test with the real observed message 'Rate limit exceeded. Only 2500 requests are allowed per 1 hour'.

sessionHistory.note: Discovery complete. Verified landed fixes (92a82ed9a, aafb1b43c) in branch; confirmed Linear poll is batched (no fan-out); confirmed #2 split to PAN-1821/1823; confirmed idle-stack-reaper has no tests. Operator chose Ask #4 + regression hardening. Authored 5-bead vBRIEF.
